ddls:
CREATE TABLE "growth_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each growth standard */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for the standard */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Standardized code for the region */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the infant */,
  "measurement_min" INTEGER /* Minimum measurement for the standard */,
  "measurement_max" INTEGER /* Maximum measurement for the standard */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the standard was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the infant */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the infant */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the infant is from */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the infant */,
  "last_visit_date" TEXT /* Date of the last visit to a healthcare provider */,
  "parental_contact" TEXT /* Contact information for the parents or guardians */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patients_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES growth_standards ("region_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient the measurement belongs to */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement taken */,
  "measurement_value" INTEGER /* Value of the measurement */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date the measurement was taken */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the healthcare provider who took the measurement */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES healthcare_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the healthcare provider */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the healthcare provider */,
  "specialty" TEXT /* Specialty of the healthcare provider */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the healthcare provider */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the healthcare provider */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time the provider last logged into the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_provider" (
  "association_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient-provider association */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the healthcare provider */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the provider started managing the patient */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the provider stopped managing the patient (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("association_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_provider_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_provider_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES healthcare_providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "growth_analyses" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each growth analysis */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the growth standard used for comparison */,
  "comparison_result" TEXT /* Result of the comparison */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date the analysis was performed */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the healthcare provider who performed the analysis */,
  "z_score" REAL /* Z-score of the measurement */,
  "percentile" REAL /* Percentile of the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_growth_analyses_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES healthcare_providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_growth_analyses_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_growth_analyses_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES growth_standards ("standard_id")
)
